description: The Combatant Clergy Association (Jame'e-ye Rowhaniyat-e Mobarez, JRM) is a conservative clerical political organisation founded in 1977 by a group of clerics including [Morteza Motahhari](/people/morteza-motahhari). It is one of the oldest and most powerful political bodies in the Islamic Republic, consistently aligning with the conservative and principlist factions. Key affiliated figures include [Ali Larijani](/people/ali-larijani) and [Ali Akbar Nategh-Nouri](/people/ali-akbar-nategh-nouri). It split in 1988 when its left wing broke away to form the [Association of Combatant Clerics](/orgs/association-of-combatant-clerics) (MRM).
editorial_notes: Founded 1977. One of the oldest political organisations in IRI. Split in 1988 — left wing formed the Association of Combatant Clerics (MRM). Key figures: Nategh-Nouri, Larijani.
